name: Rebuilt Front Brake Caliper (right) (Girling) for 76-79 Bus, 80-85 Vanagon
description:
This is a rebuilt Passenger side (right) front brake caliper for early Vanagon. There is a $75 refundable core charge. The calipers are $99 and clips and pins are  included. 

If you look at the pictures you will see that on the Girling Calipers there is a large G embossed in the center of the body of the caliper.  If you don't see this on your old one then most likely it is an ATE caliper which most of these vans had.  Please confirm this before ordering.

name: Rebuilt Front Brake Caliper (right) (ATE) for 73-79 Bus, 80-85 Vanagon
description: This is a rebuilt front brake caliper for Passenger side (right) ATE. There is a refundable $70 core charge. The calipers are $99. Pins and clip included.  These rebuilt units may or may not have the brake line relocated to the center of the caliper.  It depends on what core they are getting back.  Many times they are getting Brazil bus cores that they are rebuilding which will work but the brake line location is in the center of the caliper instead of to the side like they were in the Vanagon.  We have new calipers that have no core and have the proper line location if you don't want to deal with modifications.

name: Front Brake Caliper (left) for all 86-91 2wd Vanagon and most Syncro
description: Left front brake caliper for any Vanagon between 86-91 that has girling calipers on it. $99 plus a $54 core charge. Comes with carrier and pins. All of the 86-91 2wd Vanagons and most of the Syncro Vanagons used the Girling Caliper. So if you have a 2wd Van this is definitely the correct one for you. If you have a Syncro, my advice would be to just replace these in pairs and if you find you have ATE calipers instead you are going to have to eat the core charge. No one has any late ATE calipers that I know of. They are interchangeable as long as you replace them in pairs you are good to go. If you have ATE calipers don't even bother returning them for the core charge. It just isn't going to happen.

name: Rear Shift Linkage Cup for 83.5-91 Waterboxer Vanagon
description: Rear Shift Linkage Cup. It is at the end of the shifter rod. For 83.5-91 Vanagon waterboxer.  Many times if you have play in the shift linkage, it can be traced to this cup or the ball that goes inside of it or both.  They act as a mortar and pestle and if can dirt gets inside it will grind and grind until it wears the ball and cup and allows for more play in the shift linkage.  I would also replace the shift lever and the boot that covers it to tighten up the shifting and keep dirt out to prevent it from wearing out in the future.  The OE version of this is discontinued, I am going to start supplying the aftermarket one that is billet aluminum.  If the OE becomes available again, I will go back to that.

name: Engine to Body Seal for 80-83 Air Cooled Vanagon
description: This is a brand new aftermarket engine compartment seal that is for the 80-83.5 2.0l Air Cooled engine.  This seal goes between the body and engine tin of the Vanagon.  It helps keep the engine cool by sealing off the hot exhaust air from the engine bay.  Many people think that Air Cooled engines don't require any cooling.  They just magically get cooling from the air.  The truth is that they actually are oil cooled and they need cool air to go into the intake of the cooling system.  If the air going into the intake of the cooling system is already very hot, like say, exhaust heated air or even exhaust from exhaust leaks, then the engine is going to overheat.  It will just get hotter and hotter until you drop a valve or burn a piston.  Having a good seal of the engine bay is key to your cooling system working properly.  This seal will help ensure that your engine is cooling at it's best and reduce the chances of an overheat.
page_title: Engine to Body Seal for 80-83 Air Cooled Vanagon

name: Vanagon Front Turn Signal Lens, Left Side- Taiwan
description:
Vanagon Front Turn Signal Lens, Left side. This is the European version that has the notch between the two screws.  That is the European version and it was meant as a marker light only.  You will have to slightly modify your bulb holder to fit inside this housing.  It is a simple and easy mod.  When you try to install it you will see there is a small piece of plastic that is preventing it from seating in all the way.  Just trim that off with a sharp knife it it will work perfectly.  The original DOT version that came on our Vanagons here in the USA is long discontinued and we may never see them again.  This version only looks different if you know what to look for and you are standing 10 feet or closer.  From farther away you will never notice the difference.   Left side means driver's side in the USA.

NOTE: These are made in Taiwan but the quality is good.

Vanagon Front Turn Signal Lens, Right side for all Vanagons.   This is the aftermarket version that has the notch between the two screws.  That is the European version and it was meant as a marker light only.  You will have to slightly modify your bulb holder to fit inside this housing.  It is a simple and easy mod.  When you try to install it you will see there is a small piece of plastic that is preventing it from seating in all the way.  Just trim that off with a sharp knife it it will work perfectly.  The original DOT version that came on our Vanagons here in the USA is long discontinued and we may never see them again.  This version only looks different if you know what to look for and you are standing 10 feet or closer.  From farther away you will never notice the difference.  Especially if you order both sides, then they will match.  Right side means passenger side in the USA

These are made in Taiwan.  They are good quality.

name: Turn Signal switch for all Vanagon (black) w/o factory cruise control switch
description:
This is a turn signal switch for all Vanagon.  It is black and that is the only color that will ever be made for the Vanagon so if you need brown you are looking at either painting this one or good used (I don't have any used ones).  Also if you have factory cruise control, those controls aren't on this switch.  This will work on a Vanagon with factory cruise but it lacks the switches to control that.  As far as I know there is no source for that switch except used so check ebay and prepare to spend a lot of money.  :-(  This switch also incorporates the brass finger that is part of the horn circuit so if you are having problems with your horn, check the finger on this part.  If it is worn out and sharp, it should be replaced.

Finally, while this switch is what you move to switch between high and low beam on your headlights, this switch actually doesn't include the high/low beam switch.  That switch is part of the wiper/washer switch assembly.  So if you need a new high/low beam switch, buy the washer/wiper switch, not this one.

There will never be a brown plastic version of the switch made again. Only black. You could always paint it if you really need it to be brown. Made by JP Group in Denmark. I know it seems counter intuitive but it also has the high beam switch built into it. The other lever works it (the one for the turn signals) but the switch itself is part of the washer/wiper assembly. So if you need a new high/low beam switch this is the only way to get it.

name: A/C Compressor SD-709 for 87-92 Vanagon
description:
Brand New Compressor aftermarket-

NOTE: on the rear of the compressor you will see two holes.  On the "D" port (high side) the hole is restricted with a restricter.  This means that in order for the fitting to seat all the way into the hole you will need to shave about an 1/8th of an inch off of the end of the pipe.  Please do this and be sure that the metal plate on the fitting is flush with the head of the compressor so the o-ring will seal properly.  

A/C Compressor SD-709 These AC compressors were used on Vanagons from 86-91.  In 86 and 87, they were transitioning from the SD-508, to the SD-510, to the SD-709.  So if you have one of those two years, this compressor will work, however, some adaptation may be required.  Please check your compressor head (rear end) to be sure which version you have. SD-508 (Vanagons from 83.5-86) has compression nut fittings on the ends and looks like the picture below.

The SD-510 (86-87 only) and SD-709 (86-91) have a similar design in the end fitting, but the head itself is a different thickness which throws off how the fittings attach.  Here is a picture of the two of them side by side for reference (see below)

You can see what I mean about the thickness of the "head" of the compressor.  The other difference between the two is that the SD-709 is a 7 cylinder compressor while the SD-508 and 510 are 5 cylinder.  The higher the cylinders the more efficient the compressor.  That being said as long as you have a good functioning system, either should work well.  You should try to order what you have for simplicity sake.  If you have any questions please feel free to email us. This is a brand new compressor.  However the manufacturer is subject to change according to what we can get at the moment.  When replacing A/C compressor, flushing the system, and changing the receiver/dryer and expansion valve are required for warranty.  Warranty is 24 months.
page_title: A/C Compressor SD-709

page_description: A/C Compressor SD-709-86-92 2.1L Vanagon

name: Air Conditioning Compressor for 83-86 Vanagon (SD-508)
description:
Air Conditioning Compressor for 83-86 Vanagon (SD-508) New A/C compressor with clutch for 83-86 Vanagon. In 86 and 87, they were transitioning from the SD-508, to the SD-510, to the SD-709.  So if you have one of those two years, this compressor will work, however, some adaptation may be required.  Please check your compressor head (rear end) to be sure which version you have.  SD-508 (Vanagons from 83.5-86) has compression nut fittings on the ends and looks like this:

The SD-510 (86-87 only) and SD-709 (86-91) have a similar design in the end fitting, but the head itself is a different thickness which throws off how the fittings attach.  Here is a picture of the two of them side by side for reference:

You can see what I mean about the thickness of the “head” of the compressor.  The other difference between the two is that the SD-709 is a 7 cylinder compressor while the SD-508 and 510 are 5 cylinder.  The higher the cylinders the more efficient the compressor.  That being said as long as you have a good functioning system, either should work well.  You should try to order what you have for simplicity sake.  If you have any questions please feel free to email us. This is a brand new compressor.  However the manufacturer is subject to change according to what we can get at the moment.  When replacing A/C compressor, flushing the system, and changing the receiver/dryer and expansion valve are required for warranty.  Warranty is 12 months.

name: Air Conditioning System Blower Fan for 86-92 Vanagon
description:
This is blower fan for the rear factory AC for all (86-921L) Vanagon with factory AC.  If you have the later version of the Air Conditioning system that was installed starting in 1986 with the two blower fans then this is what you need.  There are two fans back there, so if you are having problems with your fuses blowing or wiring getting extremely hot, then you will need to order two of these to replace what you have.  If you have an 86 Westy and are wondering if you have this later system, you can look at your switches on the dash.  Do you have two switches side by side over to the right of the radio?  This you have the newer style system.  If you have the double purpose single switch to the left of the steering column, you have the older version of the AC system which this fan WILL NOT work with.

These fans look similar to the dash blower fans but they are more powerful.  These are aftermarket versions of the OE style fan, but good quality.  The OEM German fans are no longer available.

name: Cam Follower or Hydraulic Lifter for 79 Bus and 80-91 boxer engine Vanagon
description: This unit is called all kinds of things. Hydraulic lifter, Cam Follower, tappet, etc. It is for the 79 Bus and then for 80-83 Air Cooled and 85-91 Waterboxer Vanagon. OE quality. 8 per engine, 4 per side. These can get gummed up with age and they are the source of the loud tapping that folks hear when they start up their vans after sitting for a while. Sometimes they can never fully pump up and cause misfiring and rough running or idling. If you want to replace just one or two that are tapping and won't stop you can purchase our collapsible push rod tubes and just replace that one or two. The part number for those is 025109337. They allow for replacement of a lifter without removing the cylinder head.

name: Clutch Kit for 73-75 VW Bus and 83 Vanagon only 215mm
description:
Clutch Kit for 83 Vanagon only 215mm and also 3/73 to 75 VW Bus The clutch kit is for 83 1.9L Waterboxer Vanagons with a manufacture date of 3/83 or lower. These vans had a 215mm clutch. For some reason VW reached into it's dusty parts bin for this clutch kit.  They grabbed some old flywheels they had left over from the early AC pancake engine days.  So we get this clutch kit that works with 74-75 Bus and then a few months of early 1.9l waterboxer. If you have an 83 waterboxer, you should measure your clutch disc to be sure you are ordering the correct size clutch.  215mm is this oddball size.  The standard size that VW used one most every year was 228mm.

UPDATE: If you have a 73-75 Bus you should measure your clutch disc.  There was also a 210mm version of this clutch.  So you really need to measure the clutch disc before ordering.  If you just guess and get the wrong clutch, I will have to charge for additional shipping.
page_title: Clutch Kit for 83 Vanagon only 215mm
page_keyword: Clutch Kit for 83 Vanagon only 215mm, KF-22901
page_description: Clutch Kit for 83 Vanagon only 215mm-68-79 Bus, 83-85 1.9L Vanagon

name: Clutch kit for 80-91 Vanagons (2wd) (not diesel)
description: Clutch kit for 80-91 Vanagons (2wd)  Sachs Version.  Also does 76-79 VW Bus. Includes a clutch disc, pressure plate, throw out bearing, pilot bearing, and alignment tool. Always double check your flywheel for smoothness and have it machined if necessary. Also check your clutch actuating rod for straightness as this can also be a source of problems. This is the Sachs version which was OE and installed on the Vanagon from the factory.  However they also used LUK clutch kits from the factory as well and we also have them available for a higher price.  Which is better?  Sachs is fine for most applications.  LUK is heavier duty so if you have an engine conversion or an engine with more horse power I would recommend going with that. Note:  If you have an 83 1.9l waterboxer Vanagon, there were a few months in that year when VW changed the size of the flyweel, clutch disc and pressure plate to a smaller size.  Before 83, and after the clutch was the same, but for some reason they changed the flywheel and the clutch from the beginning of 83 to 3/83.  The smaller size is 215mm and the normal size is 228mm.  So if you have an 83 waterboxer, you should measure the size of your old clutch disc, just to be sure you are ordering the correct one.    If you have any other year from 80-91 you can completely disregard this note.

name: Spark Plug for all waterboxer Vanagon from 83.5-91
description:
Spark Plug for waterboxer Vanagon. The Bosch version of these has been discontinued, so these are NGKs. They will work on the 1.9l and 2.1l engine. There is a metal cap on the tip of the spark plug that needs to be removed before they will fit inside the spark plug wire. Use a pair of vise grips to remove this cap before installing. Price is for one spark plug. Always replace all four at the same time so order 4 per Vanagon you are working on. 

NGK number is BR6ES

You are buying one plug so please order 4 if you want to get a new set.

name: Oxygen Sensor Wiring Repair Kit for 2.1l Digifant Vanagon
description:
If you need to replace the wiring to your oxygen sensor from your ECU then this is the kit you need. Do you have the following symptoms: Starts and runs great first thing in the morning.  Then as you drive it, it starts to run poorly, bog down, have no acceleration, and even blow black smoke?  Then you turn it off and back on again and it runs great again for a short time and then starts doing it again? You are describing what I like to call Oxygen Sensor Syndrome and it can happen when either your oxygen sensor is bad or if the wiring from the sensor to the computer is bad.  I have found more and more, it is the second thing.  You could replace your entire engine wiring harness (we have those) or you could just repair the oxygen sensor harness.  That is what this repair harness is. How to replace it? You will have to open up your ECU plug which is pretty easy, and get the old pin out, then install this new wire from the ECU plug (under the rear seat) to the area near the coil.  Just tie wrap as you go.  You can leave the old wire in the harness, no need to pull it out, it will just be no longer used.  I would cut the ends off to avoid confusion in the future. On the 86-91 2.1l Vanagon you are looking at Pin number 2 in your harness. Once you remove the ECU plug cover you will see the numbers on the white plastic of the back side of the plug.  Pin 2 is the one you want to remove and replace with this new harness. Quick temporary fix: If you are in a pinch on a trip, you can actually cut the wire at pin 2, leave about an inch of the old wire, tape it off with electrical tape, and then continue on your trip.  The computer will use a default reading for the oxygen sensor and the van will run fine the rest of your trip.  Then once you get where you are going you can order this kit to fix it correctly or an entirely new engine harness (take your pick).

Watch our Youtube Video about how to install this here:

name: LUK Clutch Kit (Syncro too) Vanagon (Not Diesel)
description:
LUK Clutch Kit (Syncro too) Vanagon, also used on 76-79 VW Bus. Clutch Kit for all Vanagon except diesel.  Includes clutch disc, pressure plate, Throw out bearing, pilot bearing and alignment tool. These kits are made by LUK in Germany and are the same as you would buy from the VW Dealer. If you want a true German clutch for your Vanagon then the LUK kit is for you. Some Vanagons had Sachs clutch kits and some had LUK from the factory.  There doesn't seem to be much rhyme or reason to why they would use one over the other.  However the LUK kits are beefier than the Sachs so if you have a more powerful engine, I would go with the LUK kit.

This kit is the 228mm version (9 inch).  If your van is an 83-84 Vanagon you must measure your clutch disc to be sure this kit will work for you.  SOME 83-84 Vanagons had a 215mm clutch disc (8.5 inches).

name: Aluminum Thermostat Housing kit for 2wd and Syncro 2.1l Vanagon 86-92
description:
This is the lower thermostat housing for either the 2wd Vanagon or the Syncro 2.1l waterboxer from 86-92. It is made of aluminum and comes with a new temp II sensor and gauge sensor. It is an aftermarket but the quality level is excellent.  If you have a Syncro this is the exact one you should have now. 

If you have a 2wd with the original plastic housing you will need to adapt to the gauge sender as it has one pin instead of two.  You just cut the sensor plug off and splice a single female spade connector onto the yellow wire with a red stripe and you are good to go.  The brown wire is a ground so you just ignore that.  The sensor grounds through this aluminum housing instead of a separate wire.

name: Expansion Tank Cap for 83-92 Vanagon
description: Cooling System Cap for 83 and newer water cooled Vanagon.  This is the Blau cap that is the only one I sell.  Many aftermarket caps can be bad from brand new because they aren't made correctly.  If you put your mouth on the hose port and blow into it, you should get a duck call type noise and resistance.  If there is no resistance and you can just blow into it like nothing is there, with no quack sound, then it isn't going to hold pressure and work correctly.  Will not work on 82 Diesel Vanagon tank or the cube style tank that was used on some 1983 1.9l Vanagons.  You need the metal cap for those.

name: Coolant Expansion Tank for 84-85 1.9l and 84-91 Diesel Vanagon
description:
This is the coolant tank that is pressurized and it comes with a new cap. It is for 84-85 1.9l Vanagon and you can retrofit it to an 83.5 1.9l waterboxer if you are retrofitting the coolant hoses as well.  It is also the tank for the 84-91 Diesel Vanagon.  If you have the 068121405D (83.5 1.9l waterboxer) and don't want to have to fabricate anything to get this tank to mount, then you are better off buying the same part number tank that you have. This tank will work with the 83-84 Diesel Vanagon, however it may need a mount for it fabricated if your old tank was the 068121405D. If you have a 82-84 Diesel Vanagon that was manufactured before 4/83 then you need the older style expansion tank number 068121405D.

name: Fuel Pressure Regulator 83.5-92 Vanagon waterboxer
description:
Fuel Pressure Regulator 83.5-92 Vanagon waterboxer. This is the metal fuel pressure regulator that is mounted on the top of the engine in the center. It is mounted on the center of the air intake plenum, and has two fuel lines going into it from either side. It also has a small vacuum line attached. If you van is running extremely rich, this could be the cause. Pull the vacuum hose off and if gas comes out it is definitely bad. Otherwise you will have to do a fuel pressure test to be sure that it has failed.

UPDATE: Bosch has completely stopped making these and they are No Longer Available from Bosch.  The ones we are selling are aftermarket, but I have used this American company for years and they make a quality product.  You will NOT be getting Bosch but aftermarket which is the only available version now.  The supplier is Intermotor and they are made in Mexico.

name: Vanagon Automatic Transmission Oil Cooler 83.5-91
description: I have two of these aftermarket Heatsink style transmission coolers in stock at the moment.  They come with o-rings and a bypass piece so you can junction your two water cooler hoses together since you will no longer need them.  This cooler uses a heatsink, similar to what you would see inside your computer, to cool the transmission fluid instead of the oil/water cooler that was stock on the Vanagon.  The advantage of this is that it is actually a superior cooler to what was originally on the van.  Also there is no chance that in the future you will have a problem with the transmission fluid and coolant mixing inside the transmission due to oil cooler failure (yes this is a thing with the original cooler).  Finally it is the simplicity of this cooler that I like the best.  You bolt it on in the same place as the old one, and you are done.  No lines to run or remote cooler that could leak if hit by a rock or stone, etc.  Just the two normal mounting hollow bolts, and it is good to go.  There is something to be said about keeping it as simple as possible.

description: This is the Hall Effect Sensor or Sender for 79 Bus and 80-85 Air-cooled (California) and 1.9l Vanagon waterboxer (electronic ignition, no points). This parts set lets your replace the hall sending unit in your old distributor. Hall sender included. Faulty Hall sender can cause a no spark condition. This unit is no longer available in Bosch. This is an aftermarket unit that seems to work just as well. No longer comes with snap rings, shims or lube seen in picture. Recently went to purchase this from our supplier that we have been buying for from years and the price has increased dramatically! Not sure why the price increased so much in a short amount of time. If it were me I would get the rebuilt distributor versus getting this for such a price and then rebuilding yours on your own. I am keeping it on here because there will always be those folks who need to do it themselves.

description: A/C Fan Relay for 86-92 2.1L Vanagon. The center hole is 3/4" inside diameter.  If you have a question about which bearing you need measure the inside diameter of the center hole of the bearing.  This one is 3/4" as I have said but the later model one is almost an inch (15/16") in diameter so if you measure it with a ruler you should be sure which one you need before order.  This is only a question if your van is an 84.  Before that year use this one for sure, and after that year use the newer version for sure.  Link for newer version is below.

description:
These long stainless steel coolant pipes replace the long steel ones that were used on 83.5-85(early) 1.9l and Diesel 83-84.  These pipes have an outside diameter of 1.25". You do not have to drop the gas tank. If you have an 85 confirm you have metal pipes before ordering. Please check to see if your pipes are black metal (this kit) or white plastic.  If you have long plastic pipes then you need 251121399Gset instead.  The G kit pipes have a diameter of 1.5" so your hoses won't fit properly if you order the wrong pipe kit.

name: Gas Tank for 84-92 Vanagon (2wd)
description: This is a brand new aftermarket gas tank that will work for 84-92 Vanagon. If you have an 84 check the filler neck. This tank works with the plastic filler neck (50mm). See gastankkit1 as well. The outlet on this tank will fit a 12mm hose that goes directly to the fuel pump and then a metal filter goes after the pump, between the pump and the engine. Yes you can get cheaper tanks, however these are the tanks we have been using for about 15 years, the quality has been very consistently good.  I have no idea what a tank that costs less than $100 on ebay is like, but I would hate to have to do this job twice.  Don't cheap out on such a critical part!

These are made in Germany and they are stainless steel.  Stock Fuel Pump Mount.

This is a set of the fuel tank straps for the Syncro Vanagon that are made of high grade stainless steel. You really only want to do this job once in a lifetime.  The original straps were made of plain steel with a cad plating on them.  They rust like aklaseltzer plus here in the North East!  The new kit we sell replaces your original straps with German made straps that are high grade stainless steel.  Now you should never have to replace these again.  Here is a link to a write up on thesamba.com with pictures of what is involved in replacing these: https://www.thesamba.com/vw/forum/viewtopic.php?p=3695281

name: Powerflex Transmission Mount for nose of Manual Transmission Vanagon 80-85 Gasoline
description:
The transmission mount for the manual transmission air cooled, Diesel, and 1.9l waterboxer are different than the later mount that was used for everything also the automatic mount.  So if you have an early manual transmission gasoline powered Vanagon, you need a specific transmission mount for your Vanagon.  The problem is that this mount has been discontinued from any source I can find.  Thankfully the folks at Powerflex make a replacement bushing that you can use.  You need to keep your old mount, press, pound or otherwise destroy the old mount out of it.  You will still have the steel mount and the round sleeve that the new bushing will need to install into.  Installing the new bushing is very easy and it comes with lubrication for it as well.  The bushing is stiffer than stock.  The final bonus is that this bushing is much cheaper than the original or reproduction ones that we could get before it because completely discontinued.  

If you have an 85 Vanagon, you should measure your old mount.  If your mount is around 2.5" then use this one.  If it is 3 inches, then use the later model one.

 

If you have a 86-91 Vanagon or early Automatic use 251399201K.

 

Does not work on a Syncro Vanagon!

name: Rebuilt Power Steering Pump for all Vanagon
description:
This is a rebuilt power steering pump that will work with all Vanagons that have power steering.  These are rebuilt by ZF which is the original manufacturer of the pump, but it also has a Bosch sticker on it.  So I guess Bosch bought ZF, or something.

The price is $225 plus a $50 core charge.

Proper fluid and flushing the system:

Be sure you change the power steering filter when replacing this and also flush the system with fresh ATF.  The Vanagon power steering system actually uses Dexron Mercon Automatic Transmission Fluid III for the power steering fluid instead of standard power steering fluid.  Putting power steering fluid in your system will kill the seals and cause problems and leaks in the steering rack.  It will void your warranty so please be sure you use the proper fluid.  If your old fluid is clear instead of red, then you need to flush the system and install the proper fluid.  Flushing is accomplished by just unhooking the return line at the metal pipe that goes to the reservoir, putting on a longer hose that can go into a container, starting the van and filling the reservoir until you see fresh fluid coming out of the return hose into the container.  So you flush with new ATF until you see new ATF coming out.  Then you just hook things back up as normal and top off the system.  It only holds a quart and a half of fluid.  Flushing would require just an extra quart or two.

name: Rear brake drum for all Vanagon
description: If your brakes feel mushy, most of the time the problem is actually in the rear of the braking system. Or sometimes you will feel a pulsing in the pedal, it is almost always due to messed up drums, improperly installed rear brake springs, bad rear wheel cylinders, or worn out shoes. I am at the point now when I am having a braking problem with the Vanagon I always check the rear brakes first as they are the usually culprit. Be sure to look at a picture of the rear brake assembly in your Bentley manual or on this site and don't assume that your brake adjuster and springs were installed properly. name: Oil Pressure Gauge and Sender Unit
description:
Oil Pressure Gauge and Sender Unit GlowShift’s 10 Color Digital Series Oil Pressure Gauge is electronically driven and reads your engine’s oil pressure levels from 10 to 145 PSI with unrivaled accuracy. An oil pressure gauge, essential for any car, monitors and displays the vehicle’s oil pressure levels to ensure that the bearings, crankshaft and other vital engine parts stay properly lubricated. This oil pressure gauge comes included with an electronic oil pressure sensor, 9’ sensor harness, 2’ power harness, gauge visor and mounting hardware for a complete installation.   Each 2 1/16” (52mm) 10 Color Digital Gauge features a flat gauge face with a digital numerical readout for quick glance gauge readings. The digital gauge offers 10 different color options that include blue, green, red, yellow, white, teal, purple, pink, amber and orange. After the color orange, there are 2 additional modes that include the 10 color cycle and 10 color fade mode that utilize all of the featured colors. At startup, a self-calibration mode ensures that the gauge is performing optimally. Each gauge features an ultra-thin design with a depth of 1.32” and an LED display that measures at 1.05” x 0.43”. For an easier viewing experience while driving at night, connect the orange wire to the headlight switch source to allow the gauge to dim 50% when the headlights are on. GlowShift stands behind each product by including installation instructions, a one year limited warranty and free lifetime technical support.   Note: This gauge includes a 1/8-27 NPT thread size on the sensor and your vehicle may not come equipped with the same thread size. An additional threading adapter may be required for the installation of the sensor. If you are unsure if your application will require additional threading adapters, please contact a GlowShift Technical Support Specialist.  
  • 2 1/16” (52mm) Black Gauge Face with a Low Profile Tinted Lens
  • Oil Pressure Readings from 10 to 145 PSI
  • 10 Color LED Options Include Blue, Green, Red, Yellow, White, Teal, Purple, Pink, Amber & Orange
  • 10 Color LED Cycle & Fade Mode
  • 1/8-27 NPT Electronic Oil Pressure Sensor
  • 9’ Sensor Harness
  • 2’ Power Harness
  • Self-Calibration at Startup
  • Dimmer Wire Allows the Gauge to Dim 50%
  • Gauge Visor
  • Mounting Hardware
  • Installation Instructions
  • Free Lifetime Technical Support
  • One Year Limited Warranty

name: Aftermarket throttle position switch for 85-92 Vanagon waterboxer
description:
Aftermarket throttle position switch for 85-92 Vanagon waterboxer

The Hall Switch provides idle and full throttle signals to your ECU electronically, as opposed to finicky vacuum systems, or mechanical microswitches. Your old grey cam is replaced with a Magnet Disc, containing two tiny Neodymium magnets, which activate a Hall Sensor at the two throttle shaft end points. All components are pre-mounted on a double sided printed circuit board, which is tested individually with the Magnet Disc supplied with your kit. Simply remove your old microswitch, replace it with the circuit board assembly, and make two simple connections to 12V and ground with wires & connectors provided, reconnect the two wires from your old switch, and your installation is complete. We have even provided an LED to make adjusting your new switch on installation or  later much easier. Circuit is protected against over current with a resettable fuse, and isolated from your vehicles 12V system via a 5V regulator chip. All required parts, along with a detailed installation manual are included, and we are providing a FREE pocket digital VOM to the first 25 Hall TPS Kit customers. This meter can be helpful to ensure you are connecting the switch to the correct 12V point, and will be a handy trouble-shooting tool to keep in your Vanagon's glovebox. Also, a 6" T Handle 3mm Hex Wrench is included in your kit.
page_title: Aftermarket throttle position switch for 85-92 Vanagon waterboxer
page_keyword: Aftermarket throttle position switch for 85-92 Vanagon waterboxer
page_description: Aftermarket throttle position switch for 85-92 Vanagon waterboxer-83-85 1.9L Vanagon, 86-92 2.1L Vanagon

name: Gearshift Linkage bushing kit for 93-2003 Eurovan
description:
This is a simple three bushing shifter linkage rebuild kit that should help restore the shifting of your manual transmission Eurovan.

Fitting:

Some T4’s from 1990-1995 have a different pivot bush at the gearbox end compared to a T4 from 96-03 this bush is no longer available and superseded to the 96-on bush.
The technical data from VW is that you have to change the early gear linkage to the later linkage? This is not necessary! You can simply overcome this by making the borehole of the small bush supplied in our kit bigger. This can be achieved by means of a drill; you will also have to make another small hole in the side of the bush to accommodate for a roll pin that is also used to secure the bush to the early T4 linkage.

 

VIDEO:

It also goes under the block off plate on the driver's side head (towards the front of the van). It is also used on the long pipe that runs above the main pulley on the end that is under the alternator bracket. On a 2.1l it is used at the water pump for the pipe that runs under the main pulley, and under the coolant nipple that is under the alternator bracket as well. One of these gaskets is included when you purchase a water pump, however if you have a 1.9l Vanagon you will want to buy one more of these because you will be pulling the long pipe above the main pulley to replace the pump.

It has a short section of cable crimped on the front of it and a plastic bushing that is mounted above the gas tank. The front section of cable is only accessible by removing the gas tank on 2wd Vanagons. On the Syncro you can access it from below without having to remove anything. These are aftermarket, but good quality, made in the UK. They are approximately. 5ft long. name: Cylinder Head for 76-78 VW Bus 2000cc Engine
description: This is a brand new AMC cylinder head for the 76-78 VW Bus. It comes complete with valves and springs. These are made in Spain and are very high quality. The ports are oval so if you have a 79 you should confirm if you have the oval port or the D port as they were switching over to the Vanagon style head in 79 and some of them used those. These new heads do NOT use or require a head gasket so please do not install one as this can cause problems. The old metal head gaskets caused many problems with compression loss and VW actually put out a service bulletin that allowed you to omit them. The new heads are designed for the head to push up against the cylinder and that is what seals the cylinder which is better than a head gasket and won't blow as easily. I would recommend replacing these in pairs since they are a slightly different design than original.

description:
Horn Contact Ring This is the horn contact ring that pushes into the back side of the steering wheel. It provides part of the circuit path for the horn and the brass finger that is part of the turn signal switch assembly pushes against this ring. If you are having problems with your horn, this ring could be the source of the problem. You have to pull the steering wheel off and flip it over to see it. name: 9004 LED Bulb set for stock Vanagon Square outer Headlight Housing 86-92
description:
This is the 9004 LED Headlight Bulb Set for Vanagon with the stock square outer low/high beam 86-91.

This is a set of 2 headlight bulbs that you can use to replace the 9004 bulbs that came stock with the 86-91 Vanagon outer headlight.   This is just the bulbs ONLY, and NOT the outer glass housings.  They are separate.

IMPORTANT!!:  Look in the center of your outer headlight.  Does it say "Hella" then these bulbs are for you.

These bulbs are LED and much brighter than the stock lights. They also use less power so they are not as touchy about needing perfect grounds. If your wiring is old many times at the headlight, you will see a voltage drop and the lights are yellowish because of this. Relay kits can help with this issue but they are a pain for the normal person to install and can cause more electrical issues if you install them without having some skill with wiring.

Here are the specifications on the bulbs:

Max light output: 9600lm. +220% brighter than standard bulbs. Flawless beam pattern.

This kit replaces your weak, yellow, bulbs with bright white LEDs without any special wiring or relays.  Plug them in and enjoy. See our Youtube videos about them for more information.

name: Chrome Trim for Vanagon Window Seal
description:
Chrome Trim for Vanagon Window Seal 4120mm in length.  This the plastic "chrome" trim that fits in the groove of the windshield or rear hatch or side window glass seal for early Vanagon.  The windshield seal and rear hatch or side window of 80-87 Vanagons originally had this trim which usually will turn yellow and ugly with age and exposure to the sun.  The side windows are 2950 mm in length.  You just cut this trim down to the proper length you need.  It is better to have more than you need, than not enough.

This is new trim so you can make your van look nice again, however if you don't have a garage or awning to store your van under, it will fade again in a year or two, so be sure you have a place to store your van inside if you want this trim to last.  It is difficult for a lay person to install so I would also discuss the installation with your local window install person.  They should be able to do it, and have the proper tools, etc.  You are purchasing a length of 4120mm (13.5 ft) which will do either the windshield or the rear hatch window seal (2 per van).  Use chrome joining trim piece 171853339A.

name: 7 inch round LED Vanagon Headlight set
description: This is a set of 2 LED Headlight housings that will fit into the 7" Round bucket that came stock with 80-85 Vanagons and are also used on later model Vanagons that have the SA Grill Kit.  These lights are very bright but they are DOT approved, and they also have a illuminated "halo" ring that will glow white for running lights, or flash yellow along with your turn signals (additional wiring required to get the "halo" feature working though).  You can pretty much just plug and play these if you don't care about the "halo" ring feature. LED lights are very bright, and use about half of the amperage of the stock lights (even less if you are dealing with high wattage halogens).  The advantages are long life, bright, white light, and the lower amperage unloads the electrical system so that you don't need relays to save your headlight switch. The only slight modification you will have to do is bend one of the three indentations of the headlight bucket where the light sits in the bucket.  It is just a hair too big to fit as is.  I used a set of grips and then just picked the indentation that was the easiest to get to, and tweaked that about 1/32" until the light fit in snugly.  Takes just a minute and no special tools.    Watch our video to get an idea of what they are like installed:

name: Dash Camera and Back Up Camera for Bus, Vanagon, and Eurovan
description: Comes with 8GB memory card. This is a great addition to any Vanagon, Bus, or Eurovan.  The unit fits over your existing rear view mirror in just a few seconds, secured with some rubber thongs.  Then you have the option of just plugging into your 12 volt outlet on the dash, or wiring it in more permanently to 12 volt switched power.  The unit turns on with your van and then it will record both your front view and rear view simultaneously!  It can also record audio from inside the van if you wish.  It records in 1, 3, or 5 minute increments, then moves to another recording.  Eventually it will fill up the memory card (8 GB included, or you can upgrade to 32 GB), and then it will just start over and record over the unused video clips.  It is very helpful if you have an accident or witness one, or if you get pulled over by the police for no real reason (Van profiling is a real thing!) It can also be wired to your back up lights to be a back up camera.  If you put the van in reverse, the screen will shift to reverse view automatically.  You can also switch views on the fly by simply touching a button on the bottom of the unit.  It will switch to full screen front, full screen rear, picture in picture of both at the same time!  If you have a lot of stuff in the back and have a hard time seeing back there, or just want to keep an eye on bikes, etc. having the rear camera really can help a lot. Don't struggle with the small and crappy rear view mirror that has a hard time seeing around the Westy rear cabinet.  Have a record of your police "experience" just in case.  Check out our video playlist below to see what it is like installed in a Vanagon.

name: Front Carpet Set for Vanagon molded in dark Grey color
description:
7 Piece OE spec carpet set finished in luxurious tufted dark grey pile to fit left hand drive gas powered Vanagon with manual or automatic transmission.   If will work with all Vanagons from 80-92 except diesel as they have a different front floor arrangement.  The color is darker grey than the later model vans from 87-92 but it would match the lighter grey interior no problem.  Since you are replacing all of the front carpet at once I don't think it is a big deal that it is darker.  You could also use this to retrofit the grey interior into an earlier Vanagon. The picture is showing the black version for clarity sake.  The one you are purchasing is darker grey color that could have been stock in Europe.  Here the grey we got in our later model Vanagons was pretty light.  This is more a couple shades darker.  It is tufted carpet and the carpeting is a very high quality match for it as well. This carpet set is an exact copy of the factory original including all the fine details that you would expect from an OE spec carpet such as edge finishing and trim components. The kit comprises of a molded front mat with OE replica heel pad, a pair of molded wheel arch carpets that form part of the seat box, seat box side and top carpets, walk thru mat, and the small vertical step carpet. Also included is an underfelt kit as this will be required if the vehicle has no underfelt already fitted. Being a top quality labelled product it is ideal for finishing a high-end restoration project or just self-indulgence. Fitting notes: The gear lever hole is uncut but is stencil marked underneath for the option of manual or automatic transmission. We recommend positioning the carpet in place before cutting the hole to guarantee best fitment.

Comes with new underlayment/sound deadening as well.

Made in England.

name: Speedometer Cable for 1992-1996 Eurovan
description:
Speedo cable 1430mm LHD.This cable is a one piece unit opposed to the original two piece unit that Volkswagen originally supplied, comes complete with exhaust heat shielding.  

Fitting:

Fitting guide. To remove the old cable it is not necessary to remove the instrument cluster as access can be gained by lowering the fuse box cover lid, you now can reach up behind the cluster and unclip the cable. when this is done pull it though the bulkhead and unscrew the other end from the gearbox drive pinion. When refitting the cable make sure it is routed correctly with no sharp turns. The bulkhead grommet. This can be a little tricky to refit because of its location and size; the original grommet you will notice is square and the one that comes with the new cable is circular? Don’t worry it will fit, just needs a little massaging into place.

https://www.vanagain.com/article/installing-a-lithium-battery-into-a-vanagon

Get the next generation in REDARC DC to DC charging technology with the REDARC BCDC1225D In-vehicle Dual Battery Charger. It’s a 12 volt, 25A in-vehicle DC-DC battery charger designed to charge your secondary battery banks to 100% state of charge while on the move. Added features, such as a Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) solar regulator and built-in Green Power Priority (primary solar selection), means you can charge your batteries using solar panels when driving or portable panels when stationary. With simultaneous DC and Solar inputs, this makes it the most advanced dual battery charger on the market today. Have the reassurance and confidence that your batteries will be charged to their optimal levels, thanks to a unique multi-stage charging algorithm that provides specific charging profiles tailored to all common battery types. The BCDC1225D is perfect for charging AGM, Gel, Standard Lead Acid, Calcium, and LiFePO4 batteries. The unit works with 12- or 24-volt vehicle systems and with either standard or variable/smart alternators. The DC-DC battery charger has been proven to work in all types of terrains, including deep water crossings, and stands up against extreme heat (up to 176°F). It’s fully sealed against the risk of infiltration from water and dust and will hold up to the adventure at hand. Its designed to maximize the performance and life of your auxiliary battery and is suited for battery bank sizes of 75-200AH in most applications. Download the REDARC BCDC brochure here.

name: 50 amp hour lithium battery for Westfalia
description:
Please Read this Article if you are interested in installing one of these but have questions BEFORE you call or email me to ask your questions.  If you still have questions I will be glad to answer them AFTER you have read this article:

https://www.vanagain.com/article/installing-a-lithium-battery-into-a-vanagon

Lithium batteries are the wave of the future in every way.  In our Vanagon Westfalia and other small campers they offer lots of benefits and few drawbacks. This battery will fit in the battery box under the driver's seat in either the Westfalia Vanagon or stock Vanagon.    They can be used in any Camper no matter what year.  However you will also want to buy a charger that can properly charge them to get the most life and reliability out of them.  See our BCDC1225D charger for the correct charger for this battery. If you use your Vanagon camper or Westy in the way most folks do, you usually use your battery system for a few days here and there.  Unless you have your van plugged into a charger between trips, you can find your house battery dead and damaged when you are ready to use it.  The lithium battery holds its charge without being plugged into a charger for long periods of time.  It isn't damaged if it is accidentally drained down to nothing, and can be quickly recharged to full capacity. Pros: 95 percent rated amp hour usage!  Get almost the full rated use out of your battery without damaging it unlike lead acide which will become damaged if you use them below 50% their rating. No memory for charging!  Recharge to any level without damaging the battery. Charge to full capacity in just a couple of hours!  Lead acid can take a couple of days to return to full capacity without getting damaged. Lightweight! Smart battery with BMS (will prevent you from hooking it up wrong or damaging it in most cases). Extended battery life (up to 10 times the life of a typical lead acid battery). Charge loss when not actively being charged is very low (3 % per month versus 30% per month with lead acid). Built to with safety in mind, no fires or explosions due to battery thermal runaway. 5 year warranty! Cons: Can't charge below freezing. Price.  (However if it is lasting 10 times longer, this would actually be cheaper or the same cost over time). Shipping: Shipping is only available to the continental US.  Shipping is $75 flat rate for either one or two of these batteries.  They are considered a Hazardous Material and so shipping them has to go ground and is complicated.  That is why it cost so much. Please ignore all other shipping methods and choose Flat Rate for your shipping.  No the USPS $7 shipping is an error and not real so please don't choose it!  Also I can't sell these outside of the continental USA so if you are reading this from another country, or PR, AK, or HI, I am sorry but I can't ship this to you.

name: Accelerator Pedal Linkage Pivot Pin for all Automatic Vanagon
description: This is the pivot pin for your accelerator cable that is located on the side of the automatic transmission of the Vanagon.  The automatic transmission Vanagon has a strange set up for the accelerator.  It has a cable that comes from the pedal to the side of the transmission.  Then there is a lever that shifts the transmission, which the cable attaches to.  Then there is a metal rod that goes from that lever to the throttle in the rear.  If you are having problems with your gas pedal binding up, you should really be looking at this lever area on the side of the transmission (passenger side).  This pin can get corroded when the van sits and bind up so that your gas pedal feels hard to push down, and also can fail to return properly.  There is also a plastic bushing that goes along with this.  090321347,  Also check the pivot point at the end of the accelerator cable where it snaps onto the lever.  It can rust fast to the ball on the lever and then not move as it is supposed to.  This will cause the cable end to snap off over time.

description:
PLEASE READ BEFORE ORDERING!!!

This is NOT a conversion kit to change your headlights from stock DOT to H4.  These are only the bulbs that will work if you ALREADY have the outer H4 housings and want to upgrade your bulbs.  Please take a moment, look in the center of your outer sqaure headlight housings or your round housings.  Does it say "H4"?  Then you can use these bulbs.  If it says "Hella" then these bulbs WON'T work for you and you need the 9004 LED bulb kit instead.  That is here 9004 LED Bulb Kit

This is the H4 LED Headlight Bulb Set.  This H4 headlight bulb set will work on H4 square outer headlight housings that were sold on European Vanagons.  If you have this type of square headlight it will have "H4" in the center of the light.  They will also work with round H4 headlight housings that many people have used to replace the stock crappy round sealed beam headlights.  If you want more light on the subject, less stress on your electrical system, and longer bulb life, why not replace your halogens with these super bright LED bulbs?  This is a set of two bulbs.

name: Front Door Window Seal Kit for all Vanagon (no chrome trim)
description:
ATTN: THIS KIT DOES ONE (1) DOOR.  IF YOU WANT BOTH DOORS, PLEASE ORDER TWO (2).

This is the seal kit that is for the front door window seals for all Vanagon.  It is enough seals to do one door.  It can do either left or right.  You get the upper and rear side seal, the inner and outer lower scraper seals, and the front edge felt seal between the window and the vent wing.  Does not include the vent wing seal, only the seals that are in red in the pictures.  If you have a Vanagon with the chrome trim now you will need to remove it.  You can cut it with a pair of tin snips if you don't want to replace your vent wing seal right now, or you can go all the way and replace the vent wing seal at the same time and just eliminate the chrome trim all together.

The problem with the chrome trim on the front door windows is that the lower scraper seals are available again, but the upper and rear edge seal and vent wing seals that allow for the chrome is no longer available.  Not sure if they ever will be available again.  So for right now if you want to have a quiet driving experience with no wind whistling, your only hope is to go with the non-chrome trim version of these seals and the chrome trim has to go.

name: Coolant Hose Kit for 1.9l Vanagon with plastic coolant pipes
description:
This is coolant hose kit for the 1.9l Vanagon.  It is made for the Vanagon that has the long plastic pipes (late 85).  That means that the pipes are 1 and 1/2 inches in diameter.  If you have the original metal pipes (83.5-85) this kit will also work but you will need adapters (that we also sell) because the metal pipes were 1 and 1/4 inches in diameter.  Please check this when you are ordering.  If your pipes are black steel then they are the early version.  If they are white/yellow plastic then they are the later version.  Of course your pipes may have been replaced with stainless steel aftermarket long pipes as well.  In that case you should just measure the diameter to see what you have.  Here is a list of the hoses that are included:

1. Lower radiator (return) hose:
251121083H
2. Upper radiator (feed) hose:
251121082
3. Between coolant pipe (feed) and bleeder assembly:
025121062C
4. Between coolant pipe (return) and bleeder assembly:
025121102A- (We substitute another 025121062C for this one since it is NLA)
5. Between bleeder assembly (feed) and thermostat housing (lower):
025121073E
6. Between bleeder assembly (return) and pump housing (upper):
025121103C
7. Small hose between expansion tank (upper) and “H” valve:
N90166601
8. Small hose between expansion tank (lower) and “H” valve:
N90128703
9. Between cross over pipe and “H” valve (upper):
025121130A
10. Between water pump and “H” valve (lower):
025121108A
11. Short hose between two pipes on passenger side of engine:
N90128703
12. Between cross over pipe and “T” for heater core:
251265058
13. Between passenger side head and “T” for heater core:
251265056B
16. Between expansion tank cap and overflow tank:
N202811
17. Short hose between water pump and cylinder head:
N90128703
20. Small hose between bleeder assembly and cross over pipe:

N202811

The kit doesn't include the transmission cooler hoses if you have an automatic transmission.  It also doesn't include the long hoses for the heater system that runs to the front heater core, or the hoses that go from the rear heater core Ts to the rear heater core.  Those are all available, but I just do include them in the kit.

name: Filler Neck for Gas Tank for all Vanagon
description:
This is a brand new, aftermarket, gas tank filler neck that will work for all years of Vanagon.  If you have an early Vanagon from 80-84 with the metal filler neck, you should be able to use this plastic filler neck to replace the metal one, but you will also need this adapter filler neck gasket 251201139B that allows you to use the plastic filler neck with the larger diameter hole of the Air Cooled or Diesel Vanagon tank.  If you have a plastic filler neck and you want to replace it with this one you will need a few other parts as well.  Here is a list of them:

251201197

251201119D

251201131A

This is because this particular neck doesn't have the metal filler part attached to it, so it requires this other style of filler neck.  The filler is larger where you put the fuel in.  This allows for either a gasoline or diesel filler to be used with this filler neck.  So if you have an engine conversion to a diesel this would be great for you.

name: Shifter Bushing Kit for early Vanagon 80-83
description:
For many years folks have struggled finding the shifter bushings for the early Vanagon.  Many of them have been discontinued for years, but we have rounded them up or something that can be used as an equivilent, and now they are in kit form.  You are getting both bushings that are at the universal joint area above the gas tank, the two neopreme seals there, the larger bushing also above the gas tank, and the bushing that goes on the rear linkage pin.  All of these bushings in a kit so it is easier to order and you save a few dollars.  Also we have a shifter rebuild kit for the front and all of the hard parts and boots for the rear so whatever you need we have you covered.

Here is a diagram that you can look at to see the system and the parts in this kit:

Shifter diagram

The parts in this kit are numbers: 4, 21, 22, 26, and two 31.

Will work on all Air-Cooled Vanagon and also Diesel Vanagon in 1982.  Later diesels were all 5-speed in the USA and used the newer style bushings.

page_description: Shifter Bushing Kit for early Vanagon 80-83, 80-83 Air Cooled Vanagon, 82-84 Diesel Vanagon

name: Early Vanagon Rear Shifter Hard Parts Kit for 80-83
description:
If you an early Air Cooled or Diesel Vanagon you know how difficult it can be to find the rear shifter parts.  Actually I know because for years folks called us asking for them and we had to tell them sorry they are no longer available.  Then I decided to do something about it so we had the guide pin and rear shifter remade!  Now you can restore the shifting of your early Vanagon to like new without jimmy rigs!  This kit includes the rear two ball shift lever, the rear linkage guide pin, and the boot for the guide pin.  At a discount of what you would pay for all of these separately.  We also have a kit for the bushings and another for the front shifter.  So if you want your early Vanagon to shift like new again, this kit is a way to achieve that.  

You can see a diagram of the shifter system so you can see where these parts fit into the system.

Shifter Diagram

The parts in this kit are numbers: 15, 17, and 23 on the chart.

If you want the other two kits there are below:

Front Shifter Rebuild Kit

Center Shifter Rebuild Kit

The kit works with all Air Cooled Vanagons, and also 1982 Diesel Vanagon.  in 1983 and newer models of the diesel, they used the later style shift bushings and linkage.

name: Vanagon H4 Square Headlight Kit with LED bulbs
description:
Here is what is included:

The left and right outer H4 headlight housings and inner H3 housings mounted on a backing plates with new adjusters.

A set of boots for the rear of the housings.

A set of H4 LED bulbs for the new housings

A set of adapter wiring plugs so you can just plug your new lights into your existing harness.

A set of H3 LED bulbs for the inner lights.

Read the description and watch the video below, but understand that what is above is what we currently have for sale.

This is a product that we have been waiting on for a few years now.  The square headlights have been discontined for many years as a new part.  On top of that the original DOT approved lights that were stock on all later model Vanagon here in the USA were made to diffuse light.  This was supposed to help not blind on coming traffic, but it made your lights from the driver's stand point, dim and unfocused.  However in Europe they were fine with an H4 housing that had brighter bulbs available and also focused the light so the driver could actually see better (what a concept).  These were considered the best headlights you could get for your late model Vanagon.  However since the square headlight in Europe was actually rare, these were always had to come by.  Fast forward to now, we have been working with a supplier to reproduce the great H4 square lights and we are finally ready to start selling these as a full kit.
page_title: Vanagon H4 Square Headlight Kit with LED bulbs

page_description: Vanagon H4 Square Headlight Kit with LED bulbs 86-92 2.1l Vanagon

name: Rubber Bellows Tube between body and front door all Vanagon
description: This is the rubber bellows boot that goes between the front door and the body on all Vanagon that have power items in the doors.  So if you have an early Vanagon with no power windows, or mirrors, you won't have these boots.  But if you have a later model Vanagon with power stuff in the door, you will have this boot between the front door and the body.  It is located in the door hinge area and a bunch of electrical wiring is inside.  The problem can be when someone has cut the boot open to work in the wiring.  This is the wrong approach to fixing the wiring.  The best way I have found is to pull the wiring out, do the repairs on either side of the hinge area, but not in the hinge itself, and then put the wiring back inside the tube.  You are buying one tube.  One is required for each side.  I am going to list them for early Vanagons too just in case someone has added power items to them, as it will fit, but it wasn't stock on any Vanagon before 1986.

name: Adapter set for H4 Headlights from 9004 Vanagon Bulb
description:
If you want to add H4 headlights to your later model Vanagon, one of the problems is that the plug that your van has now is a "D" shaped plug that is for the 9004 bulb.  This is true of all of the Vanagons from 86-92 that had the square headlights.  You may be changing to the South African Grill kit with an outer H4 round light.  Or you may be upgrading to the new square H4 headlights that are being made.  Normally you would have to buy a pair of new H4 plugs and then cut off your old plug, and splice on the new plug.  This isn't that big of a deal if you have the proper tools and some skill in making eletrical connections.  However a better option would be to just plug in this conversion harness and get the same result.  No fuss, no muss, and you are done in a few seconds.  You can always reverse what you have done if you wish to go back to original (like if you are planning to sell the van, but want to keep your upgraded lights for your next van, etc.).  Just unplug and put the old lights back.

This set of plugs will make that upgrade much more plug and play.  You plug the blue plug into your existing wiring harness and the other three prong plug into your new lights and you are done.  You are buying a pair of these plugs so they will do both outer headlights on your van.  Why not make this switch as simple and easy as possible?

If it were me, I would order this front shifter kit as well, that way everything is new in the front shifter.

name: Hall Sending Unit for 80-85 Vanagon Distributor with digifant style plug pigtail.
description:
The hall sending units for the California Air Cooled 2.0l Vanagon from 80-83.5 L-jetronic and the 83.5-85 1.9l Vanagon with digijet fuel injection, have been very difficult to get for a while now.  However we did find that a kit is available, but it has the newer style digifant plug on it.  You will have to modify the aluminum body of the distributor to install the new plug as it is slightly bigger.  You will need it cut the notch in the side of the distributor it a little wider and a little deeper to allow the new plug to seat.  The original notch in the side of the distributor is 7/16 wide by 9/16 deep.  The new style plug requires a notch that is 5/8" wide by 5/8" deep.  So just slightly wider and deeper.  You should be able to do this modification with a dremel tool in just a few minutes.  The kit comes with a new style plug for the engine harness that you can splice in.  You are are not only replacing your hall sender but also the digijet wiring harness plug which can also get sketchy with time.  Let me know if you have any further questions about doing this mod.

See picture for doing the modification.  You want to grind equally on all three sides (blue outline).

name: Front Door Power Lock Actuator for all Vanagon
description:
The front door power lock actuator or motor for the Vanagon is no longer available from any source that I know of.  However the Eurovan power door lock actuator will work and VW actually sold this as a replacement for the Vanagon with a conversion wiring harness to allow you to plug it into your existing harness.  We supply you with the aftermarket Eurovan door lock actuator and the Vanagon harness as a kit so you can replace your old actuator.  The old one is black and the new ones are smaller and green.  They look different, but they fit and work the same.  If you have a green one already with the adapter harness let us know and we can sell you just the actuator.  If you have the original actuator, then you will need this kit.  

FYI: many times the problems folks have with the front door electrics like the power door locks, the power mirrors, and the power windows, are because of broken wires in the front door jamb either on the driver's side (most common) or the passenger side or both.  Investigating these areas before buying new electrical items is usually the best course of action.  You will see a black, accordian boot going from the door to the body at the front edge in the hinge area.  The wires are inside of this boot.  Pull one end of the boot and look for broken wires (pull on them to see if the broken one will pull out).

name: Rear Window Seal for Vanagon Sinka or Doka
description:
This is the rear window seal for the Vanagon pick up truck.  This is called a Sinka (Single Cab) or a Doka (Double Cab).  This seal can be tricky to replace.  I highly recommend not removing it yourself and using a professional glass installer to remove the old seal and install the new one.  There is a locking bead on the inside of the vehicle . The glass is not a rope in window. you leave the rubber on the body and install the glass into it . Once in the rubber ,you then use a locking tool and install the locking spline .   If you destroy this seal with improper install you are going to be responsible for buying a new one to replace it.  I am not responsible for damage due to improper install.

I may have this in stock or I may have to order it.  If you are in a rush please contact us before ordering to see if we have it in stock at the moment.

name: Barebones Dash AC Kit for all Vanagon
description:
This is the barebones Dash AC Kit that will give you some of the parts that you will need to install dash AC in your Vanagon.  It is a bare bones kit because there are other items and add ons you will need, however they are very specific to the Vanagon that you have, what engine you have, etc.  These parts are kind of universal so you can use them no matter what.  The kit includes the dash AC unit, a trianary pressure switch, a new drier, and a control panel.  Once you purchase this kit you I would recommend a new condenser which you can buy separately, and you will want to get all new AC hoses made up.  These hoses are custom lengths so I don't include them in the kit.  You can get a local AC shop to make them for you once you install the dash unit and measure things.  I would also recommend getting a new compressor.  We have Vanagon compressors available if you are installing this kit in a wasserboxer Vanagon.

These kits are made to order so please allow a couple of weeks for delivery.  

If you wonder, should I buy this kit, or not please read this article I wrote before asking me further questions:

Vanagon AC Words of Wisdom

name: Head Gasket for 1.7l Diesel Vanagon  2-notch
description:
This is the 2 notch version of the 1.7l NA Diesel Engine that came in some European market Vanagons from 87-92.  If you have a USA market diesel Vanagon they only had 1.6l NA or even Turbo Diesel engines.  However in Europe this used a 1.7l NA engine from 87-92 in many vans and trucks.  Before you place your please be sure you have the KY engine code.  This is the 2 notch version of the gasket so you will also have to check to see if that is the correct thickness of the gasket that you need.  If you look behind the fuel pump you will see a place where the head gasket sticks out from under the head near the center of the engine.  You will see notches or holes in the head gasket there.  Count them and then order your new gasket accordingly.

NOTE: This is a special order item.  We may or may not have it in stock.  Please check before ordering.

name: LED H4 Headlight Kit for 92-95 Eurovan
description:
This is an upgrade kit for your Eurovan.  It includes a pair of H4 headlight housings that are mounted to plastic frames, along with city bulbs, city bulb sockets, and 9004 bulb to H4 bulb adapter wiring harness, and H4 LED bulbs.  Your original headlights were DOT approved housings that used 9004 bulbs.  These housings were made to scatter light and not blind on coming traffic.  However it goes too far in this and the DOT housings scatter light so much that the driver only has a dim light on the road.  Not very safe for the driver of the van.  

In Europe they used H4 housings instead.  These help focus the light on the road where you want it.  We have been selling LED bulbs for H4 housings for the last couple of years and really like them.  They are brighter, whiter light, while drawing less amperage from the system, thereby unloading your switch, and wiring system.  The LED bulbs have a two year warranty, but should last for 10 years or more.  This kit comes with an adapter wiring harness that allows you to just plug these H4 bulbs into your stock wiring with no problems.

These will work on Eurovans from 92-1995.  After 1997 they used a different light housing which we also have available.

Note: Because these lights were never stock here in the USA I have to say that these are sold for off-road use only.  However they were legal and stock in Europe for many, many years.

If you want to put more light on the road and see better at night, these lights will do the trick.
page_title: LED H4 Headlight Kit for 92-95 Eurovan
page_keyword: LED H4 Headlight Kit for 92-95 Eurovan
page_description: LED H4 Headlight Kit for 92-95 Eurovan-92-96 2.5l Eurovan

name: B-Pillar Air Duct Seal in front Door Jamb all Vanagon
description:
This oval vent is located on the rear edge of the front door.  It is located on either side on a passenger Vanagon.  It's purpose is to allow fresh air to be channeled to the rear of the van so that it can come out at the interior roof vents.  All passenger Vanagons will have these vents on both sides.  If you open the front door and look near where the door latches you should see an oval hole where this vent is located.  

NOTE: if you have a Westfalia, you will have one of these vents on the passenger side and nothing on the driver's side.  This is NORMAL.  There is no air passing through that side due to the cabinetry, so the air flow is blocked with a block off plate in the front door jamb.  

If you have a single cab or double cab, these vents should also not exist.  So if you think you need one or two of these because there is just a hole with nothing, please note that if it isn't a passenger van, it may have never had this.

name: Flasher Module for use with LED Turn Signal Bulbs all VWs
description: If you want to use LED bulbs in your front turn signals or rear flashers, you are going to run into a problem.  The original flasher unit used the resistance of the bulb filament as a way to see if your bulb is good or bad.  If it is burnt out, you get the quick flash that tells you there is a problem.  Because of this, when you switch to the LED bulb (which has no filament) it messes up the way the original flasher unit works.  So now it won't flash!  You have two choices.  You can get some resistors, and do a bunch of soldering.  Or you can buy this flasher unit, remove the old one, install the new one and you are good to go.

name: Non Power Rear hatch lock with keys 84-92 Vanagon
description:
This is the larger version of the rear hatch lock that became standard after 1984.  If you have an 84 take a look at the lock.  If it is flush with the hatch and you have to push it in and it is about the size of a 50 cent piece then this is the lock you need.  If you it is more like a button that sticks out then you need the earlier version that was used from 80-84.  This is an aftermarket lock with two keys.  You may be able to use your old lock cylinder from your old lock to key this alike to keep all of your keys the same, but this lock cannot be rekeyed as the tumbler is different than the original.  So you would need to see if you can swap the entire tumbler.

Also this lock doesn't have the provision for the power lock mechanism on it so if you have that and really want to keep it, you can't use this lock.  However if you want to eliminate the power rear hatch lock and just lock and unlock the rear hatch with the key (never get locked out again), this is a good way to go.

name: Coolant hose shield for lower thermostat housing on 1.9l Waterboxer Vanagon
description:
This shield is for the lower coolant hose that is near the exhaust on a 1.9l Vanagon.  This shield protects that hose from getting cooked by the exhaust which is around 800 degrees at the pipe.  There is an air gap, but this shield deflects most of the heat radiation prolonging the life of your hose.

 

PS Before you ask, no the later model Vanagons don't require this.  ONLY for the 83-85 1.9l Vanagon.

PS, These covers WERE NOT used on earlier Vanagons.  If you have an 80-85 Vanagon, you aren't missing this cover, there never was one and there isn't a practical way to add one.  They had a different version of the fuse panel that wasn't covered.  PLEASE READ this before you purchase this cover.

name: Clutch Kit for 72-73 VW Bus with 210mm Clutch
description:
This is the clutch kit that is for the very early VW Bus with the 1700cc type IV engine.  It should work on 1700cc Bus engine from 8/71 to 12/73 build date.  The clutch is 210mm (8 and 1/4 inches) in diameter.  It comes with a clutch disc, pressure plate, and throw out bearing  If you have a 74 Bus you should check your build date, and even then I would recommend pulling the engine and measuring the clutch before ordering.  The 74-75 version has a 215mm clutch disc and then after that VW used a 228mm clutch disc for late bus and all Vanagon (except diesel and a few months in 1983 on the 1.9l).

Your flywheel must be machined, or the warranty is not valid.

UPDATE:  This kit is only available as a Chinese Version.  The one in the picture is Sachs and you will get everything in the picture but you will not get Sachs, only a Chinese maker instead.

also known under the part number of 022198141
page_title: Clutch Kit for 72-73 VW Bus with 210mm Clutch

name: Pop top Westfalia roof canvas, T3 8/1985-7/1992, grey, 3 windows, high quality
description:
Grey Canvas that will work with tops that have the aluminum mounting strips in the top.  Not for use with the plastic strips at the top.

Top quality canvas. It is a beautiful reproduction, very close in quality to the original ones, made from a cotton fabric of the same thickness, with three windows, allowing a great amount of light inside.
Grey colour, as the Westfalia original, with 3 windows and mosquito nets.
The canvas is treated to be totally waterproof on the outside and breathable from the inside. Excellent water repellent properties (water slips without penetrating).
The fabric has a high tear resistance.
The zippers are specially designed to facilitate their passage in tight corners.

BEWARE:
Before removing the existing roof canvas, and before the installation of the new canvas, please verify that the measurements between the two are rigorously identical. Despite the information given on our website, there is always the possibility that your canvas or your roof does not correspond to the year or model indicated.
No credit will be issued for canvasses that have been either totally or partially installed.
Replacing the canvas is no easy task. The operation requires the technical skills of an upholsterer. Additionally, you will need to remove the roof. No instructions included. We advise you to have it installed by a professional.
Do not forget to also change your roof seals with the GVW231720 kit.

Make sure that the canvas matches your vehicle. As the model year is not a foolproof indicator, pay attention to the way it is installed. From 5/1979 to 7/1985 canvasses were stapled at the top, and screwed at the bottom base. In models from 8/1985 to 7/1992 they were screwed at both top and bottom parts.

name: Engine Wiring Harness for Instrument Cluster 86-92 2.1l Vanagon
description:
There are two wiring harnesses in the engine compartment for the 86-92 2.1l Digifant powered Vanagon.  The big one is for the fuel injection and the smaller one is for the sensors that give you the readings for your instrument cluster.  This harness is the second, smaller one. 

It has the wiring and plugs for the coolant temp sensor for the gauge (both styles of plug), it has the plug for the oil pressure sending unit wiring (not the actual brown wires that plug into the sensors, but the wiring that plugs into that), it has the plug for the coolant level sending units in the tank (both styles of plug), the wiring for the tach signal on the cluster, the wiring and plug that powers the fuel pump and ECU relay box, and the wiring for the heater for the crank case vent hose. 

We also sell a larger harness for the fuel injection system of the Vanagon.  That can be found here:

Digifant Harness

name: H4 Outer Headlight Housing for 86-92 Vanagon- Left
description:
Left means Driver's Side in the USA.  This is made for left hand drive vehicles.

This is a brand new outer headlight housing that is a replacment for the H4 headlights on a Vanagon.  Please understand that the Vanagons sold here in the USA had DOT headlight housings that uses the 9004 bulb.  This housing is the European version that uses the H4 bulb.  I comes with a H4 halogen bulb.  This looks the same as the DOT light housing from a distance but it is different.  The bulb is different, the lens of the light is different, and it is not just a plug and play install if you have the stock lights.  You will also need a wiring adapter to make this work.  I would also not recommend replacing just one of these lights.  It will be much brighter and look weird at night if you only replace one side of these.  If you already have the H4 lights, then this is a good replacement for one of those.  

NOTE: How can I tell what type of light housings I have?  The easiest way is to look at the center of the light.  On the DOT lights (not this one), it will have the word "Hella" made into the glass.  If it is this version which is H4 it will say "H4" in the center of the glass.  Please visually check this before ordering.  If you order the wrong one, and need to exchange it, you will have to absorb those costs.

name: Aftermarket Rebuilt Alternator for Vanagon waterboxer 83-91
description:
This is a rebuilt 90 amp alternator that will work with all waterboxer engine Vanagon, 83-91.  So 1.9l and 2.1l.  It has a two year warranty. The price is $99 plus a $30 core charge

 

These are Bosch units that have been rebuilt.  They are cheaper than the OEM Bosch remanufactured, as these are just rebuilt.  There is a difference between rebuilt and remanufactured.  Rebuilt is checking and replacing parts as needed.  Remanufacturing is replacing everything no matter what and bringing it back to original new condition (or even better in some cases).  So this is the rebuilt unit, the price is less than a remanufactured unit.  I haven't had any failures of these, but the lower price is due to it being rebuilt.  I hope this helps clear this up.

name: Diesel Vanagon Oil Filler Conversion Kit for 82 Diesel
description:
The 82 Diesel Vanagon has lots of one year only things that can be a pain to get if they break or go bad.  One of the bigger problem child parts is the oil filler tube.  The original one was almost all plastic and a dipstick in the center of the cap.  The filler can crack, the dipstick is plastic and can break.  If either of these happen, there are no new parts available.  Finding good used ones is pretty much impossible.  Our solution?  Install the filler tube and dipstick parts from the 83 and newer Diesel and Turbo Diesel Vanagon!  All of these parts are available as new ones at the moment.  Here is a list of what is in our kit:

Oil Filler Tube

Seal for Oil Filler

Oil Filler Cap

Dipstick Tube

Dipstick

Dipstick Tube small plastic tube

Dipstick tube o-ring 

The new dipstick is metal and goes into a metal tube that goes into the block.  You will need to remove a plug that is in the block to install the new dipstick tube.  Beside that, the install is a bolt on.
